demographics.summary: Priya teaches chemistry at Lincoln High. Routine: bike commute Mon/Wed/Fri, weekly lab prep on Sundays. Switches to a new curriculum (NGSS-aligned) mid-week. Has a peanut allergy and a son named Rohan.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Monday morning. Biked into Lincoln High, took the Springwater Trail like always. Setting up the stoichiometry unit today. The kids hate moles week.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Long day. Need to finish lab prep tonight, the titration setup is tricky. Rohan was up late on his game so I had to confiscate the controller.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Hope tomorrow's smoother.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Wednesday bike commute. Light rain but manageable. Period 3 chemistry, I'm using the Lincoln High curriculum we've used for years.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Acknowledged.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Department head meeting after school. Heads up: the district is mandating we switch to NGSS-aligned curriculum next semester. Big change.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: That's a major shift.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Trying to read up on stoichiometry titration techniques for Friday's lab. The new NGSS curriculum has different lab guidance. Frustrated.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Noted.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Friday bike day. Rohan packed his lunch with peanut butter again, I had to swap it out — peanut allergy in our house, you'd think he'd remember. Bike ride was nice, no rain.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it. Allergy noted.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Today's titration lab went OK actually. Some students confused molarity with molality but we got there. Decided we're starting NGSS-aligned curriculum on Monday. I'm switching today.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Big change going through.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Quick rant before bed: NGSS-aligned curriculum is what we're doing now. The old Lincoln High curriculum is officially shelved as of today.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Acknowledged the switch.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Sunday lab prep day. This is my weekly ritual — get out the glassware, prep solutions, plan the week's experiments. I do this every Sunday afternoon at the kitchen table.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Productive Sunday.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Five hours of NGSS lesson planning done. Need to revisit the inquiry-based teaching approach the new curriculum keeps mentioning.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Noted.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Sunday lab prep, same routine. Week 1 of NGSS-aligned curriculum done, still adjusting. The kids' inquiry questions are sharper than I expected.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Glad it's going OK.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Going to dinner with Rohan tonight. Reminded the restaurant about my peanut allergy ahead of time, they were great about it last week too.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Glad they're attentive.

probe_queries.query: What weekly patterns have you noticed in my schedule?
Which technical terms I've used recently are you unsure about?
Which curriculum am I currently teaching?
Which curriculum was I using previously?
What should Rohan avoid packing in his lunch?

cross_session_questions.query: What should Rohan avoid packing in his lunch?

author_notes: Priya the chemistry teacher. Tests cross-domain pattern (Mon/Wed/Fri biking + Sunday lab prep), professional jargon gap (titration), and contradiction (curriculum switch mid-week). Cross-session question requires combining 'Rohan packs peanut butter' (day 3) with 'allergy reminder at restaurant' (day 5).
